mcqs.question: What is the primary cryptocurrency used on the Ethereum network?

options.text: Ether (ETH)
Ethereum Classic (ETC)
Bitcoin (BTC)
Litecoin (LTC)

mcqs.explanation: Ether (ETH) is the primary cryptocurrency used on the Ethereum network.

mcqs.question: What is the name of the virtual machine used to execute smart contracts on the Ethereum network?

options.text: Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)
Java Virtual Machine (JVM)
Python Virtual Machine (PVM)
C++ Virtual Machine (CVM)

mcqs.explanation: The Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is used to execute smart contracts on the Ethereum network.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of gas in the Ethereum network?

options.text: To measure the amount of computational effort required to execute a transaction or smart contract
To measure the amount of data stored on the blockchain
To measure the number of transactions per block
To measure the number of nodes on the network

mcqs.explanation: Gas is used to measure the amount of computational effort required to execute a transaction or smart contract on the Ethereum network.

mcqs.question: What is the consensus mechanism used by Ethereum?

options.text: Proof-of-Work (PoW)
Proof-of-Stake (PoS)
Delegated Proof-of-Stake (DPoS)
Leased Proof-of-Stake (LPoS)

mcqs.explanation: Ethereum uses a proof-of-work (PoW) consensus mechanism.

mcqs.question: What is the world state in the context of the Ethereum network?

options.text: The current state of the Ethereum network, including all accounts, balances, and smart contracts
The current state of the blockchain, including all blocks and transactions
The current state of the network, including all nodes and connections
The current state of the virtual machine, including all executing contracts

mcqs.explanation: The world state is the current state of the Ethereum network, including all accounts, balances, and smart contracts.

mcqs.question: What is a contract creation transaction?

options.text: A special type of transaction used to create a new smart contract on the Ethereum network
A type of transaction used to execute a function on a smart contract
A type of transaction used to send Ether from one account to another
A type of transaction used to deploy a new node on the network

mcqs.explanation: A contract creation transaction is a special type of transaction used to create a new smart contract on the Ethereum network.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Ethereum stack?

options.text: To provide a framework for building decentralized applications
To provide a framework for building smart contracts
To provide a framework for interacting with the Ethereum network
To provide a framework for understanding the different layers of the Ethereum network

mcqs.explanation: The Ethereum stack provides a framework for understanding the different layers of the Ethereum network.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a hard fork and a soft fork?

options.text: A hard fork is a change to the underlying protocol that is backward compatible, while a soft fork is a change that is not backward compatible
A hard fork is a change to the underlying protocol that is not backward compatible, while a soft fork is a change that is backward compatible
A hard fork is a change to the underlying protocol that requires a node upgrade, while a soft fork is a change that does not require a node upgrade
A hard fork is a change to the underlying protocol that is temporary, while a soft fork is a change that is permanent

mcqs.explanation: A hard fork is a change to the underlying protocol that is not backward compatible, while a soft fork is a change that is backward compatible.
